questions.question: Which tool built into most browsers helps you inspect and debug HTML and CSS?
questions.explanation: Browser Developer Tools (opened with F12 or right-click > Inspect) let you view the HTML structure, inspect CSS rules, and debug issues live.

questions.question: What is a common mistake when writing CSS that causes styles not to appear?
choices: Forgetting the semicolon at the end of a declaration
questions.explanation: A missing semicolon after a CSS declaration can cause that rule and subsequent rules in the same block to be ignored by the browser.

questions.question: Why is it best practice to use external CSS files instead of inline styles?
choices: External files allow reusing styles across multiple pages and are easier to maintain
questions.explanation: External stylesheets promote separation of concerns, allow style reuse across multiple pages, and make maintenance much easier.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a best practice in HTML?
choices: Always closing tags that require closing
Using only <div> and <span> for all elements
questions.explanation: Using only <div> and <span> ignores semantic HTML. Best practice is to use meaningful tags like <header>, <nav>, <article>, and <footer>.

questions.question: You can open browser Developer Tools by pressing the ___ key.
questions.explanation: Pressing F12 in most browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Edge) opens the Developer Tools panel for inspecting HTML and CSS.

questions.question: CSS stands for Cascading Style ___.
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. The 'cascading' part refers to how styles are applied based on specificity and source order.
